project.creator: Bao
environment: 3. Evidence source: client/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx, client/src/shared/components/figma/landing.tsx, client/src/constants/index.ts, and UC-02 description in UC-Des.html
4. Execution evidence: no dedicated landing-page automated test/spec was found in the repository
project.notes: Feature workbook for FN-03 Public Landing Content. Cases are derived from the implemented landing-page wrapper and presentational component behavior and are currently marked Untested.

changeLog.description: Initial function-level workbook generated from the implemented landing page rendering and navigation behavior.

features.name: Public Landing Content
features.testRequirement: Validate guest access to the public landing route, static section rendering, sign-in navigation, sign-up navigation, and mobile menu toggle behavior for FN-03 Public Landing Content.

functions.name: LandingPage
functions.description: Route wrapper that binds public landing interactions to application routes.
functions.preCondition: Guest user opens the root route and the client bundle loads successfully.

functions.name: LandingPageComponent
functions.description: Presentational landing page that renders hero, feature, role, workflow, CTA, and footer content.
functions.preCondition: The component receives valid sign-in and sign-up callbacks from the route wrapper.

testCases.description: Render the public landing page successfully for an unauthenticated guest.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the application root route /.
2. Allow the landing bundle to render.
3. Observe the visible page sections.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The route resolves to the public LandingPage.
2. The landing UI renders hero, features, roles, how-it-works, CTA, and footer sections.
3. No authentication gate or redirect blocks the guest user.
testCases.dependencies: Browser can reach the client app and the root route is configured to ROUTES.LANDING.
testCases.note: Derived from App route mapping and LandingPage component rendering.

testCases.description: Navigate to sign-in from the desktop landing actions.
testCases.procedure:
1. Render the landing page in desktop layout.
2. Click a sign-in action such as the navbar Sign In button or the hero Sign In button.
3. Observe router navigation.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. handleNavigateToSignIn invokes navigate(ROUTES.LOGIN).
2. The client routes to /login.
3. No intermediate error state is shown.
testCases.dependencies: Desktop navigation is visible and ROUTES.LOGIN is configured.
testCases.note: Covers the sign-in callback wired through LandingPage.tsx.

testCases.description: Navigate to sign-up from the landing CTA actions.
testCases.procedure:
1. Render the landing page.
2. Click a sign-up action such as Get Started, Start For Free, or Create Free Account.
3. Observe router navigation.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. handleNavigateToSignUp invokes navigate(ROUTES.REGISTER).
2. The client routes to /register.
3. The action works from each wired CTA button.
testCases.dependencies: ROUTES.REGISTER is configured and the CTA button is visible.
testCases.note: Covers every shared sign-up callback target in the landing component.

testCases.description: Open the mobile navigation menu from the hamburger button.
testCases.procedure:
1. Render the landing page in a mobile viewport.
2. Click the mobile menu toggle button once.
3. Inspect the menu state.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. mobileMenuOpen changes from false to true.
2. The animated mobile menu becomes visible.
3. Feature, For You, How It Works, Sign In, and Get Started actions are displayed.
testCases.dependencies: Mobile layout is active and the menu toggle button is available.
testCases.note: Derived from useState-driven mobile menu branch in landing.tsx.

testCases.description: Close the mobile navigation menu after selecting an anchor item.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the landing page in a mobile viewport.
2. Open the mobile menu.
3. Click a section anchor such as Features or For You.
4. Inspect the menu state after the click.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The clicked anchor remains available for in-page navigation.
2. setMobileMenuOpen(false) executes.
3. The mobile menu collapses after selection.
testCases.dependencies: The mobile menu is already open.
testCases.note: Covers the explicit close handlers attached to mobile anchor links.

testCases.description: Keep the public landing content accessible without backend data dependencies.
testCases.procedure:
1. Inspect the rendered landing flow and its direct dependencies.
2. Load the root route without performing authenticated API requests.
3. Observe whether the page can still render.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. LandingPageComponent renders static marketing content, local assets, and callback-driven buttons only.
2. No required backend fetch blocks initial display.
3. Guests can browse public information before signing in or registering.
testCases.note: Matches the implemented static-content-first design in landing.tsx.
